_meta.purpose: Genre conventions for ForgeFlow Games automated 2D action pipeline. Each genre object documents the minimum requirements for a 'recognizable, playable example' of the genre.

platformer.identity: Side-view, gravity-driven traversal where a character runs and jumps across discrete platforms toward a level exit, with falling and enemy contact as the primary failure modes.

controls.keyboard: Arrow keys or WASD = move (Left/A and Right/D primary; Down/S = duck/drop-through), Space or Up/W = jump (variable height: hold for max), Shift = run/dash, Z/J = attack (if combat platformer), X/K = special/secondary, P/Esc = pause
controls.mouse: Not required. Optional aim for projectile-attack platformers (Hollow Knight uses gamepad-style 8-dir, not mouse).
controls.gamepad: Left stick or D-pad = move, A/Cross = jump, X/Square = attack, B/Circle = dash/run, Y/Triangle = special, Start = pause

win_conditions: Reach level exit (flag, door, sign post)
Defeat boss at end of level
Collect all required collectibles in some sub-modes
lose_conditions: Fall into pit / off bottom of screen

standard_enemy_types.name: patroller
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Walks left-right between edges, dies when stomped on (Goomba archetype)

standard_enemy_types.name: shell_walker
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Armored walker; stomp retracts into kickable shell that becomes a hazard (Koopa archetype)

standard_enemy_types.name: flyer
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Sinusoidal or hover patrol in air, requires aerial combat or dodge

standard_enemy_types.name: shooter
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Stationary or slow patrol; fires projectiles on a timer

standard_enemy_types.name: charger
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Idle until line-of-sight, then rushes the player

standard_enemy_types.name: jumper
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Hops in place or in arcs to block traversal

standard_enemy_types.name: boss
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Multi-phase HP gauge, telegraphed attacks, weak window

iconic_mechanics: Variable jump height tied to button hold duration
Stomp-to-kill enemies from above (Mario-style head-bounce)
Coyote time + jump buffer for forgiving jumps
Ring/coin shield: take a hit, scatter rings, no death (Sonic)
Momentum preservation through slopes and loops
common_bugs_anti_patterns: Jump cuts to zero velocity instantly on key release (no taper) — feels broken; correct fix is to dampen upward velocity, not zero it
No coyote time: jumping one frame after walking off ledge fails — players blame the game
No jump buffer: pressing jump 2 frames before landing eats the input
Resetting velocity by re-adding to physics group every frame — bullets/enemies stutter
Using physics.matter for a simple platformer — overkill, slower, harder to tune
Gravity applied via setVelocityY instead of setGravityY — frame-rate dependent
Forgetting world bounds → player falls forever instead of dying
One-way platforms with two-way collision blocking jump-up
Enemy patrollers without ledge-detection raycasts walk off platforms
platformer.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade (NOT matter) for snappy feel. Tilemap with collision layer for level geometry (setCollisionByProperty or setCollisionBetween). Player as Sprite with arcade body, setGravityY on world or body. enemies = physics.add.group({allowGravity:true}). add.collider(player, layer) and add.collider(player, enemies, hitEnemy). Use blocked.down for ground detection. Particles for coin sparkle and stomp puff.

arcade.identity: Single-screen, twin-stick or single-stick shooter where the player survives escalating waves of enemies in a bounded arena with no scrolling.

controls.keyboard: WASD = move, Arrow keys = aim/shoot direction (twin-stick on keyboard), Space = bomb/special, Shift = boost (optional), P/Esc = pause
controls.mouse: Optional alternative to right stick: mouse position = aim, left-click = shoot, right-click = bomb
controls.gamepad: Left stick = move, Right stick = aim+shoot (auto-fire while pushed), RT/R2 = bomb, LT/L2 = boost

win_conditions: Survive all waves to high-score threshold
Endless mode: highest score before death
Defeat wave boss every N waves
lose_conditions: Touched by enemy or enemy bullet

standard_enemy_types.name: grunt
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Walks/drifts toward player in straight line, dies in 1 hit

standard_enemy_types.name: shooter
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Maintains distance, fires aimed bullets at player

standard_enemy_types.name: splitter
standard_enemy_types.behavior: On death, splits into 2-4 smaller faster enemies (Geometry Wars green diamond)

standard_enemy_types.name: swarmer
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Spawned in groups, weak individually but overwhelm (Geometry Wars purple)

standard_enemy_types.name: chaser_smart
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Pathfinds around obstacles, persistent threat (Robotron Hulk archetype)

standard_enemy_types.name: indestructible
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Cannot be killed, must be avoided (Robotron Hulks)

standard_enemy_types.name: rescuable_humanoid
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Non-enemy NPC, contact = bonus score, killed by enemies = lose

iconic_mechanics: Decoupled move-and-shoot (twin-stick): aim while strafing in any direction
Score multiplier that climbs with kill-streak and resets on hit
Spawn-in waves with brief invulnerability flash
Smart bombs as panic-button screen clears with limited charges
Bullet-time / boost meters that fill from kills
common_bugs_anti_patterns: Bullets created without object pool → GC stutter at high fire rates
Resetting bullet velocity inside physics group iteration
Aim direction calculated from mouse but not normalized — diagonal bullets travel sqrt(2) faster
Spawning enemies on top of player at wave start — unfair instant deaths
No invulnerability frames after respawn
Score multiplier stored as float and decays mid-frame causing jitter in HUD
Bombs that don't actually destroy off-screen bullets
Splitter enemies spawning their children at exact dead position, causing chain-overlap damage
arcade.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade with setCollideWorldBounds(true) on player. bullets = this.physics.add.group({classType: Bullet, runChildUpdate: true, maxSize: 200}) with bullets.get() / killAndHide pattern for pooling. enemies = physics.add.group with custom AI in update. Particles for explosions (this.add.particles). Camera shake on bomb (this.cameras.main.shake). Pad input via this.input.gamepad.

shmup.identity: Auto-scrolling shooter (vertical or horizontal) where the player moves freely in 2D while the camera/world drives forward, dodging dense enemy bullet patterns and downing waves and bosses.

controls.keyboard: Arrow keys or WASD = move (4 or 8 dir), Z = primary fire (auto-fire while held), X = bomb/special, Shift = focus/slow-mode (smaller hitbox, slower move), P/Esc = pause
controls.mouse: Not standard. Some Western shmups support mouse-aim but it breaks bullet-pattern memorization design.
controls.gamepad: Left stick or D-pad = move, A/Cross = shoot, B/Circle = bomb, RB/R1 = focus mode, Start = pause

win_conditions: Complete all stages and final boss
lose_conditions: Touched by any enemy or bullet (one-hit-kill on player hitbox)

standard_enemy_types.name: popcorn
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Trash mobs in formations, 1-hit kills, fire 1-2 bullets each (Galaga grunts)

standard_enemy_types.name: formation_diver
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Holds position then dives at player in arc (Galaga attack pattern)

standard_enemy_types.name: turret
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Stationary on scrolling background, fires aimed shots until destroyed

standard_enemy_types.name: tank
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Heavy mid-boss, multiple HP, complex bullet pattern

standard_enemy_types.name: kamikaze
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Rushes player position in straight line

standard_enemy_types.name: boss
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Multi-phase, bullet-pattern attacks, large hitbox, HP bar visible

iconic_mechanics: Auto-fire so player focuses on dodging
Tiny visible hitbox vs large sprite (focus mode reveals it)
Memorizable bullet patterns (danmaku); skill = pattern recognition
Power-up tiers that escalate weapon spread/damage, lost on death
Capture/rescue (Galaga): boss tractor-beams ship; rescue gives dual fighter
Polarity switching (Ikaruga): match polarity = absorb, mismatch = damage
common_bugs_anti_patterns: Player hitbox = full sprite size — felt unfair vs visible-bullet spec; fix: shrink body to 4-8px square center-of-sprite
Bullets spawned with physics body but no maxVelocity → frame-rate scaling issues
Enemy patterns built with setTimeout instead of scene timer — pause breaks them
Scrolling background moved by camera scroll but enemies not in same coord space → desync
Power-up sprite collidable with bullets, gets shot off-screen
No bullet-pool: 200 bullets/sec creates 200 sprites/sec, GC death
Boss HP bar reads from dead reference after boss exit
shmup.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade. Player body shrunk via setSize() and setOffset() to tiny hitbox. TileSprite for scrolling background (this.add.tileSprite + tilePositionY += scrollSpeed). bullets pool with physics.add.group, maxSize: 500. Enemies spawned via Phaser.Time timer events on stage timeline. Boss HP bar = Graphics rect redrawn each frame. Use scene.tweens for bullet-pattern arcs.

flight.identity: Top-down or 3/4-perspective scrolling combat-flight game with banking/turning ship feel, scrolling terrain, and missions structured around objectives or boss encounters (1942, Aero Fighters, Tyrian).

controls.keyboard: Arrow keys or WASD = pitch/yaw (Up=accelerate or pitch-down, Down=decelerate or pitch-up depending on convention), Z = primary gun, X = bomb/missile, Shift = boost/afterburner, C = roll/barrel-roll (Star Fox-style), P/Esc = pause
controls.mouse: Optional aim (cursor = lead reticle); not standard for arcade flight
controls.gamepad: Left stick = move plane, A = fire, X = bomb, RT = boost, LB/RB = barrel-roll left/right, Start = pause

player_abilities: weapon swap (cannon vs spread vs laser)
win_conditions: Complete mission objective (escort, defend, destroy fleet)
lose_conditions: Mission objective failed (escort target died, time expired)

standard_enemy_types.name: fighter
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Single small plane, weaves toward player, fires aimed bursts

standard_enemy_types.name: bomber
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Slow, heavy HP, fires straight or drops bombs

standard_enemy_types.name: ground_aa
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Anti-aircraft on terrain, tracks player, fires flak

standard_enemy_types.name: ship
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Naval target, multiple turrets, scrolls slowly

standard_enemy_types.name: missile
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Homing projectile, must be dodged or shot down

standard_enemy_types.name: ace
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Mid-boss, evasive maneuvers, signature paint

standard_enemy_types.name: boss_battleship
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Stage-end large target, multiple destructible parts before core

iconic_mechanics: Banking sprite frames (3-5 frames: hard-left, left, neutral, right, hard-right) tied to horizontal velocity
Barrel-roll i-frames that deflect bullets
Loop maneuver to flip 180 (in non-rail flight)
Wingmen that can be lost (Star Fox: 'Falco, where are you?')
Mission-based level structure with branching paths (Star Fox map)
common_bugs_anti_patterns: No banking animation — plane feels static and stuck
Boost has no fuel limit → flies off ahead of camera
Ground vs air enemies use same collision layer → fighter killed by tank
Missile lock without warning audio → feels random
Camera scroll speed not matched to player forward velocity → terrain rubber-bands
Barrel-roll i-frames last whole animation including landing frame → exploit
Mission objective marker doesn't update on minimap
flight.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade. Player Sprite with frame-swap on velocity.x for banking. Scrolling TileSprite for terrain/clouds (multi-layer parallax: tilePositionY += speed). Enemies in physics groups split by air/ground tag. Minimap = second Camera (cameras.add) at scaled view. Missile homing via Phaser.Math.Angle.Between + setVelocity per frame.

obby.identity: Stage-based obstacle course (Roblox-popularized) where players traverse a sequence of platforming challenges separated by checkpoints, with death sending player back to the last checkpoint instead of game over.

controls.keyboard: WASD or Arrows = move, Space = jump, Shift = sprint (optional), R = reset to last checkpoint, P/Esc = pause/menu
controls.mouse: Camera control in 3D obbies; not used in 2D obby variants
controls.gamepad: Left stick = move, A/Cross = jump, X/Square = reset, Start = pause

win_conditions: Reach the final stage / Winner's Room
Touch the goal pad / final checkpoint
lose_conditions: Fall off the world / into the void
Time runs out (rare, only timed-obby variants)

standard_enemy_types.name: kill_brick
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Static hazard tile (lava, spikes, void) — instant death on touch

standard_enemy_types.name: moving_platform
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Patrols path; not enemy but as much a hazard if missed

standard_enemy_types.name: disappearing_block
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Vanishes after step or on timer, forces speed-traversal

standard_enemy_types.name: swinging_axe
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Pendulum hazard requiring timing

standard_enemy_types.name: saw
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Spinning blade on rail, periodic kill-trigger

standard_enemy_types.name: pusher
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Piston / wall that knocks player into void

iconic_mechanics: Checkpoints every 3-5 obstacles that update respawn point and play a positive jingle
Death is cheap and frequent: instant respawn at last checkpoint, no lives system
Stage progression with named themes (lava world, sky world, etc.)
Difficulty curve: simple jumps -> moving parts -> disappearing blocks -> timed combos
Trolling/joke stages as a sub-genre signature
common_bugs_anti_patterns: Checkpoint script doesn't validate sequence — player skips stages by touching a far checkpoint first
Respawn places player inside collision geometry → instant re-death loop
Kill-brick collision uses overlap but logic in collide → 1-frame intersect bypasses
Disappearing blocks regenerate while player still standing on them → throws player
No jump buffer / coyote time — feels worse than Roblox itself
Camera doesn't follow respawn cleanly, jarring snap
obby.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade like a platformer. Checkpoint = overlap zone (physics.add.overlap) that writes player.lastCheckpoint = {x,y}. Kill-brick = overlap that calls respawn(). Moving platforms via tweens or setVelocity with reverse on bounds. Disappearing blocks = sprite with body.enable toggled on timer. R-key handler in scene update. Stage transitions can stay in one Game scene with checkpoint-driven camera bounds.

topdown.identity: Overhead 4 or 8-directional movement game emphasizing exploration and tile-based combat (Zelda LttP, Pokemon, Hotline Miami) where the player navigates rooms/screens populated with enemies, NPCs, and puzzles.

controls.keyboard: WASD or Arrows = 8-dir move, Space or J = primary attack/sword, Shift or K = secondary/shield/dash, E = interact/pickup, Q = inventory or item-swap, P/Esc = pause/menu
controls.mouse: Optional aim (cursor = facing direction). Hotline Miami uses mouse to face/aim while WASD strafes.
controls.gamepad: Left stick = move, A = attack, X = interact, B = dodge/dash, Y = item, RB/LB = cycle items, Start = menu

player_abilities: face/aim (separate from move when mouse used)
win_conditions: Defeat all enemies in room (lock-in style)
lose_conditions: Spotted by stealth enemy in fail-state mission (rare)

standard_enemy_types.name: patroller
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Walks fixed path, hits on contact (Zelda Octorok)

standard_enemy_types.name: shooter
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Stops, faces player, fires projectile (Zelda Octorok rocks)

standard_enemy_types.name: charger
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Detects on line-of-sight, rushes player (Hotline thug)

standard_enemy_types.name: armored
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Blocks frontal attacks, must be flanked or stunned

standard_enemy_types.name: spawner
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Stationary; emits weak adds until destroyed

standard_enemy_types.name: ranged_sniper
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Long sight-line, telegraphed shot (Hotline shotgun cop)

standard_enemy_types.name: boss
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Locked-room encounter, weak point cycles, phase shifts at HP thresholds

iconic_mechanics: Room-based level design: enter screen, sometimes door locks until cleared
Overworld vs dungeon distinction with separate music and tilesets
8-direction sprite system: 4 cardinals minimum, 8 ideal, with attack animations per facing
Push-block and torch-light puzzles in dungeons
Item-based progression gates (Hotline: weapons drop in level; Zelda: bomb opens cracked walls)
One-shot lethality (Hotline) vs hearts (Zelda) as a tonal lever
common_bugs_anti_patterns: Diagonal movement = sqrt(2) faster than cardinal (forgot to normalize velocity vector)
Sprite facing not updated when only y-axis input held → looks frozen
Attack hitbox active for whole animation including recovery frames
Camera follows player past room edge → revealed unintended areas
Enemy pathfinding uses raycasts every frame → CPU spike with many enemies
Z-ordering: player drawn under tree they're standing in front of
Knockback applied as velocity then immediately overwritten by movement input
topdown.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade. Player velocity normalized via Phaser.Math.Vector2.normalize() then *speed. Tilemap with collision layer. Anim keys per direction (walk_up, walk_down, walk_left, walk_right; or 8-dir if needed). Attack hitbox = transient Sprite or Zone added to physics group, destroyed after N ms. Z-sort via depth = body.bottom for player vs prop layering. Camera.startFollow with deadzone.

arpg.identity: Top-down combat-and-loot game where the player kills enemies, collects randomized gear, and grows in power through skills and item stats (Diablo lineage; Hades adds run-based roguelite structure).

controls.keyboard: WASD = move, Left-click = primary attack/move-to-cursor, Right-click = secondary skill, 1-4 = ability hotkeys, Q = potion/heal, E = interact, Tab = inventory, Esc = pause/menu
controls.mouse: Required. Mouse position = facing/aim. Click-to-move (Diablo) or WASD-strafe with mouse-aim (Hades, Path of Exile 2) — both are standard variants.
controls.gamepad: Left stick = move, Right stick = aim, A = primary attack, B = dodge, X = secondary, Y = special/cast, RT = ultimate, LT = consumable, D-pad = ability cycle

win_conditions: Defeat final boss / clear final area
lose_conditions: HP = 0 (run ends in roguelite, respawn at checkpoint in non-roguelite)

standard_enemy_types.name: trash_melee
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Skeleton/zombie pack, weak HP, swarms (Diablo Fallen)

standard_enemy_types.name: ranged_caster
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Stationary or kiting, fires projectile/spell

standard_enemy_types.name: elite
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Tougher trash with affixes (fire-aura, vampiric, fast)

standard_enemy_types.name: champion
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Mini-boss with name plate, drops better loot

standard_enemy_types.name: summoner
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Spawns trash adds; priority kill

standard_enemy_types.name: boss
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Phased, telegraphed AoE, drops guaranteed unique

hud_elements: ability hotbar (1-4 + Q/E icons with cooldown overlays)
item rarity color glow on ground
iconic_mechanics: Loot rarity tiers with color coding: white < blue (magic) < yellow (rare) < orange/gold (unique/legendary)
Cooldown-gated skills shown on hotbar with sweep overlay
Health/mana globes drop from kills and replenish on pickup
Ability synergy / build crafting: passives + gear + skill combos
Loot explosion on boss kill (Diablo loot pinata)
Roguelite room-by-room reward choice (Hades boons)
common_bugs_anti_patterns: Cooldown timer keeps running while game paused
Loot drops created without depth-sort → items rendered under floor tiles
Click-to-move pathfinds through walls due to A* misconfig
Damage numbers stack at exact same pixel → unreadable
Crit chance rolled per damage instance instead of per attack → multi-hit weapons feel wrong
Mana cost deducted after cast succeeds, not before → can cast at 0 mana
Inventory grid that allows item overlap on drag-drop
Loot magnet/pickup radius applies through walls
arpg.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade for collision. Tilemap or hand-built room layouts. Player attack via add.zone + physics.add.overlap with enemy group. Skills in a SkillManager class with cooldown timers. Floating damage numbers via this.add.text + tween fadeOut. Loot items as Sprites with rarity-tinted glow (Graphics or particles). Inventory in a separate Scene launched via scene.launch (overlay).

action.identity: 2D top-down combat-focused action game (Hotline Miami, Enter the Gungeon) where fast movement, aim, and one-or-few-shot lethality combine in tight rooms — distinguished from arpg by absence of loot/skill-tree progression and by emphasis on twitch reflex over build crafting.

controls.keyboard: WASD = move, Mouse = aim, Left-click = shoot/attack, Right-click = throw weapon / lock direction (Hotline) or active item (Gungeon), Space = dodge-roll (Gungeon) or pickup/melee (Hotline), R = reload, F = pickup, Q = swap weapon, Shift = sprint (optional), Esc = pause
controls.mouse: Required. Cursor = aim direction; player faces cursor at all times.
controls.gamepad: Left stick = move, Right stick = aim, RT = shoot, LT = active item, A = dodge-roll, B = reload, X = pickup, Y = swap, RB/LB = cycle weapon, Start = pause

win_conditions: Clear all enemies in current room/level
Reach exit door (unlocks after room clear)
lose_conditions: HP = 0 (often 1-2 hit kills for Hotline tone; Gungeon has hearts)

standard_enemy_types.name: thug_melee
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Rushes player with bat/knife (Hotline thug, Gungeon Bullet Kin melee variant)

standard_enemy_types.name: pistol_grunt
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Stops, fires aimed shots, low HP (Bullet Kin / Hotline cop)

standard_enemy_types.name: shotgunner
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Close-range high-damage spread; dangerous in tight rooms

standard_enemy_types.name: armored
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Heavy HP, slow, fires heavier weapon

standard_enemy_types.name: fast_swarmer
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Low HP but fast, comes in groups, flanks

standard_enemy_types.name: boss
standard_enemy_types.behavior: Multi-phase room boss with bullet patterns + minion summons

hud_elements: HP/hearts (or single life indicator for Hotline tone)
iconic_mechanics: Mouse-aim decoupled from move direction (twin-stick on KBM)
Pickup-and-throw weapons: every weapon is also a thrown projectile (Hotline)
Dodge-roll i-frames (Gungeon) — i-frames during the roll, vulnerable on recovery
Reload-as-skill: timing/managing reload mid-combat
Room lockdown: doors close until all enemies cleared, then unlock with thunk
Score-multiplier combo (Hotline): chain kills with no pause, A+ rank as goal
common_bugs_anti_patterns: Bullet inherits player velocity unintentionally → moving sideways while shooting curves bullets
Aim line drawn from sprite center but bullet spawns at gun-tip → bullet path doesn't match preview
Dodge-roll i-frames not actually disabled on hitbox, just ignored in damage func — overlap calls still queue
Room-clear check fires before last enemy's death animation completes → door opens with corpse mid-air
Reload triggered while throw animation playing → soft-lock state
Weapon swap mid-reload duplicates ammo
Picked-up weapon retains physics velocity → flies away after pickup
Combo timer pauses with menu, but combo bar doesn't render that way — visual desync
action.phaser_implementation: physics.arcade. Player Sprite with rotation = Phaser.Math.Angle.Between(player, mousePointer.worldX/Y). Bullets pooled in physics.add.group, spawned at gun-tip offset (rotated). Dodge-roll = state machine: set body.checkCollision.none = true for N ms, restore. Room as a Phaser.Geom.Rectangle; enemy count tracked; on 0, tween door sprite open and emit room_clear. Weapons as data + sprite; pickup via overlap + key press.
